name: GitHub CodeQL Static Analysis
what_it_does: Semantic code analysis engine and query language — enabling developers and security engineers to find security vulnerabilities in source code by treating code as data and running QL queries against a compiled CodeQL database, with GitHub Actions integration for CI/CD scanning, SARIF output for tool integration, and CodeQL CLI for local analysis across C/C++, C#, Java, Kotlin, Go, Python, Ruby, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Swift.
use_cases: Scanning source code for security vulnerabilities via CodeQL in GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines
Writing custom CodeQL queries to detect application-specific vulnerability patterns
Finding injection vulnerabilities (SQL, XSS, path traversal) via CodeQL data flow analysis
Auditing open-source dependencies for security issues via CodeQL database analysis
Integrating SARIF vulnerability results into GitHub Security tab via CodeQL
not_for: Runtime security monitoring (CodeQL is static analysis — use Falco or Datadog for runtime)
Binary-only scanning without source code access
SCA/dependency scanning without writing code queries (use Snyk or Dependabot)
best_when: A development team needs deep semantic SAST with custom query capability — where CodeQL's data flow analysis finds complex vulnerability patterns that regex-based tools miss.
avoid_when: You need runtime protection, don't have source code access, or need pure dependency vulnerability scanning without code analysis.

auth.notes: GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT) or GitHub App token for GitHub Code Scanning API. CodeQL CLI runs locally — no API auth needed for local analysis. GITHUB_TOKEN in Actions workflows for automatic integration. GitHub Code Scanning REST API at api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/alerts. SARIF upload via GitHub API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for public repos; GitHub Advanced Security required for private repos
pricing.paid_tiers: GitHub Advanced Security: $49/user/month (or included in GitHub Enterprise)
pricing.notes: Acquired by GitHub from Semmle (UK) in 2019. CodeQL CLI: free and open source (MIT for query libraries). GitHub Code Scanning: free for public repos. Private repos require GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) license. CodeQL databases for major open-source projects available at lgtm.com (discontinued) and CodeQL.com. Supports: C/C++, C#, Java/Kotlin, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Ruby, Go, Swift. Data flow analysis (taint tracking) is key differentiator vs grep-based tools. SARIF 2.1 output format for integration. GitHub Actions codeql-action for CI/CD. Community query packs via GitHub.

performance.rate_limits: GitHub API: 5,000 req/hr authenticated; CodeQL database build: depends on codebase size

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GitHub-owned SAST engine. CodeQL CLI + GitHub Code Scanning API. MIT license for query libraries. GitHub Advanced Security for private repos. SARIF output. Semantic data flow analysis. Excellent documentation.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Alert reads idempotent by alert number. SARIF uploads create or update alerts idempotently. Database builds are deterministic for same source.
known_agent_gotchas: Two interfaces: CodeQL CLI (local) vs GitHub Code Scanning API (cloud) — different workflows
Private repo scanning requires GitHub Advanced Security license ($49/user/month)
CodeQL database must be built from source — binary-only projects cannot be analyzed
Query execution time scales with codebase size — large repos can take hours
SARIF upload API: POST to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/sarif — base64-encoded gzip
alert_number is the ID for Code Scanning API — not a UUID or monotonic across repos

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (GitHub HTTPS). GitHub PAT with security_events scope for Code Scanning API. GHAS for private repo scanning. Source code transmitted to GitHub for cloud scanning. SOC2/ISO27001 certified. MIT license for query libraries (auditable). Data flow analysis finds complex vulns. SARIF output enables tool-chain integration.
